title: sync container state before reading the healthcheck
body:
The health check result is stored in the container state. Since the
state can change or might not even be set we have to retrive the current
state before we try to read the health check result.

Fixes #11687

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I made the opposite change around two years ago, to fix another bug. I'll try to find the commit for the exact justification, but AFAIK it had something to do with forcing a refresh of the state every time it was used, for safety.
https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/1db70cce344724e091635bc8c6fa6c207450df68 was where I swapped this over. Attempting to determine if the concerns there are still relevant.

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/1168#issuecomment-408702202

This still seems valid - I don't think we can safely retrieve containers from the DB in all circumstances if we also grab the state.

@mheon So if I understand this correctly, we can never assume the state is there or still valid. If we need to use a field from the state we have to call `ctr.snycContainer()` to get a fresh state, correct?
OK I think this is better now. I just sync the state before we read the health check result.
